title: Uniface Mobile Apps
text: Uniface Mobile Apps A Uniface mobile app is essentially a wrapped web application, so it consists of a hybrid mobile app that provides the client-side user interface and functionality, and a Uniface runtime environment that executes server-side components and reads and writes data in a database. This type of architecture is commonly know as a packaged hybrid app . It enables you to develop the same code for all platforms, but take advantage of native functionality. The client consists of a wrapper around a web-based user interface provided by dynamic server page (DSP) components. The app can access the device functions it needs (such as camera and geolocation) via JavaScript APIs provided by plug-ins. The wrapper is generated by a hybrid app framework, which enables you to specify the target mobile platforms, the plug-ins required to access device features, as well as the initial web page to be displayed. Figure 1. Uniface Mobile App Architecture Because Uniface mobile apps are wrapped web applications, they share the same infrastructure and communication as web applications. For more information, see Client-Server Communication in DSPs . DSPs for mobile apps make use of a layout framework that controls how content, menus, and navigation tools are defined for rendering on multiple devices, and a mechanism for binding these to DSP components. For more information, see Mobile App Layout . To run the web server side of the mobile application, you can define a customized application server for the mobile app backend. For more information, see Application Shells for Servers . Related Links Developing a Mobile App Uniface's mobile solution is based on web technologies, so mobile development starts with creating a responsive web application that is tailored to the possibilities and limitations of mobile devices.
